extra.reasoning:
The property (monotonicity in rash) is VIOLATED. Only rule r5 references rash, and it is pathological: r5 has priority 100 (far above all others) and sets acuity to self_care (the LOWEST acuity), firing only when rash is true together with palpitations, dizziness, severe_headache, chest_pain, and vomiting.

Counterexample: palpitations=T, dizziness=T, severe_headache=T, chest_pain=T, vomiting=T, leg_swelling=F, dyspnea=F, slurred_speech=F.

With rash=FALSE: r5 does not fire. Matching rules include r2 (chest_pain, prio 3 -> emergent), r9 (dizziness, prio 3 -> emergent), plus several prio-2 urgent rules. Highest priority is 3, so acuity = emergent.

With rash=TRUE (all else identical): r5 now fires because all six of its conjuncts hold. r5 has priority 100, which beats every other matching rule, so acuity = self_care.

Setting rash true thus lowers acuity from emergent to self_care, directly violating the requirement that setting rash true never lowers acuity. The witness_assignment gives the rash=true input that produces the lowered (self_care) outcome relative to its rash=false counterpart (emergent).
